name: Alaska
image_alt: Plump green pea pods on short vines
description: One of the earliest-maturing shelling peas, producing plump pods on compact 2-3 foot vines. Round, smooth seeds with a sweet, classic pea flavor. Thrives in cold, wet conditions that stall other varieties.
history: An heirloom documented since 1880, bred for far-north climates where the growing season is short and cold. The name reflects its adaptation to Alaska-like conditions — it will germinate in near-freezing soil.

good.reason: Classic companion — peas fix nitrogen for carrots

seasonal_quirks: Sow as early as soil can be worked — even in frozen ground in mild winters. Germination at 40°F. Stops producing when heat arrives — plan succession or treat as a single-harvest spring crop.
harvest_cues: Pods should be round and plump, filling the pod completely. Shell and taste — peas should be sweet, not starchy. Entire plant harvest at peak.
variety_notes: Best choice for cold, wet spring climates. Smooth seeds (not wrinkled) indicate cool-temperature adaptation — wrinkled types need warmer soil to germinate reliably.
preservation: Blanch and freeze — peas freeze exceptionally well. Dry for split peas.
